---
title: "fesm-utils"
subtitle: "Shared Fortran utilities and vendored libraries for fast Earth system models"
toc: false
---

**fesm-utils** is a convenience repository holding a self-contained Fortran
utility library together with the external C/Fortran libraries needed to build
and run fast Earth system models such as
[CLIMBER-X](https://github.com/cxesmc/climber-x) and
[yelmox](https://github.com/palma-ice/yelmox).

Everything is driven from a single root `Makefile`, generated per machine by
[configme](https://github.com/fesmc/configme). A serial and an OpenMP variant
build side by side (`openmp=0` / `openmp=1`).

## What's inside

The repository bundles the local **utils library** (`src/`, compiled into
`libfesmutils.a`) plus three vendored **external libraries**, each in its own
container folder with the upstream source and compiled `serial`/`omp` install
prefixes.

### Local packages

::: {.pkg-grid}

::: {.pkg-card}
### [coords](packages/coords.qmd)
Coordinate geometry, map projections, Gaussian grids, kdtree, and grid-to-grid
weight-map remapping (nearest-neighbour / bilinear / conservative) with CDO
interop and map caching.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [tsgen](packages/tsgen.qmd)
Transient scalar forcing from time (ramps, sinusoids), tabulated files, or
model-response feedback controllers.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [series](packages/series.qmd)
Lightweight tabulated time-series reader (ASCII/netCDF), scalar or
multi-channel, with clamped-linear interpolation.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [varslice](packages/varslice.qmd)
Slice gridded (x-y-z-time) variables out of netCDF files, with sub-annual axes,
multi-file matching, and unit scaling.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [ncio](packages/ncio.qmd)
High-level netCDF input/output for Fortran — create files, dimensions,
variables, and attributes with minimal boilerplate.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [nml](packages/nml.qmd)
Read and write Fortran namelist parameter files, with a defaults overlay and
validation.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [root_finder](packages/root_finder.qmd)
Scalar root finding via Newton and secant iterations.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [timing](packages/timing.qmd)
Wall-clock timers, output-write scheduling, and timestep management.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [grid operations](packages/gridops.qmd)
Spatial derivatives, distances, and subgrid interpolation — plus Arakawa-grid
staggering (work in progress).
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [gaussian_quadrature](packages/gaussian_quadrature.qmd)
Finite-element Gaussian quadrature over 3D hexahedral elements.
:::

::: {.pkg-card}
### [foundations](packages/foundations.qmd)
Precision kinds (`wp`/`sp`/`dp`) and package-wide constants every module builds
on.
:::

:::

### External libraries

Three vendored upstream libraries, each built by its own autotools
`configure`/`make`/`make install` into `<lib>/<lib>-{serial,omp}`:

- **[FFTW](external/fftw.qmd)** — the Fastest Fourier Transform in the West.
- **[LIS](external/lis.qmd)** — Library of Iterative Solvers for linear systems.
- **[SHTns](external/shtns.qmd)** — fast Spherical Harmonic Transforms.

## Installation

fesm-utils is built through [configme](https://github.com/fesmc/configme), which
is the single source of machine/compiler truth (Fortran flags, netCDF
autodetection) and generates the root `Makefile`.

### 1. Install configme

```bash
pip install configme      # or: pipx install configme
```

### 2. Install fesm-utils

Clone, configure, and build in one step — select your machine and compiler:

```bash
configme install fesm-utils -m macbook -c gfortran
```

In an existing checkout, just (re)generate the `Makefile`:

```bash
configme config fesm-utils -m macbook -c gfortran
```

### 3. Build with make

One consistent interface, selecting the variant with `openmp=`:

```bash
make fesmutils-static openmp=0    # the utils library -> include-serial/libfesmutils.a
make fftw   openmp=0              # serial FFTW  -> fftw/fftw-serial
make lis    openmp=1             # OpenMP LIS   -> lis/lis-omp
make shtns  openmp=0             # serial SHTns -> SHTns/shtns-serial
make libs   openmp=0             # fftw + lis + shtns, one variant
make usage                       # list the main targets
```

::: {.callout-tip}
Build the external libraries **serially** (a one-time autotools build — do not
pass `-j` to `make libs`). SHTns links the FFTW built here, so `fftw` must be
installed before `shtns`. On clusters, `module load` your compiler + netCDF
toolchain first — the builds and netCDF autodetection use the environment
already loaded in your shell.
:::

## Using the libraries downstream

Symlink the repository into a downstream model and reference the install
prefixes directly:

```bash
cd climber-x/
ln -s /path/to/fesm-utils ./
```

- utils: `fesm-utils/include-serial` (or `include-omp`)
- FFTW: `fesm-utils/fftw/fftw-serial`
- LIS: `fesm-utils/lis/lis-serial`
- SHTns: `fesm-utils/SHTns/shtns-serial`
